summary: WebSocket Real-time Notification System testing completed. All core WebSocket features are working correctly. Backend tests: 7/7 passed (100%). Frontend tests: WebSocket connection, 'Live' status indicator, and notifications dropdown all working.

minor.issue: Initial WebSocket connections fail with code 1006 before succeeding on retry. This appears to be a timing/race condition but the reconnection logic handles it correctly.

passed_tests: WebSocket status endpoint /api/ws/status - Returns connection counts by user type
Manager login returns valid JWT token for WebSocket authentication
Notifications endpoint - Returns list of notifications with priority field
Mark single notification as read - Works correctly
Mark all notifications as read - Works correctly
Dashboard stats endpoint - Returns properties, units, tenants counts
UI: Manager login flow - Successfully navigates to dashboard
UI: WebSocket connection establishes - Shows 'Live' status after initial retries
UI: WebSocket status indicator in header - Shows 'Live' with green/emerald styling
UI: Notifications dropdown - Opens and shows 'Live' indicator with notifications
UI: Dashboard loads correctly - Stats cards visible with correct data
critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket implementation is well-structured with connection manager supporting multiple user types (manager, contractor, tenant, admin)
Reconnection logic with exponential backoff is implemented correctly in frontend
Initial WebSocket connections sometimes fail with code 1006 but retry mechanism works - this is acceptable behavior
Notification priority system (critical, important, info) is implemented with visual indicators
seed_data_used: Existing test data from previous iterations

websocket_feature_status.frontend_live_indicator: ✅ Working - Shows 'Live' with green styling in header
websocket_feature_status.notifications_dropdown: ✅ Working - Shows 'Live' indicator and notifications with priority

context_for_next_testing_agent: WebSocket real-time notifications are fully implemented and tested. The WebSocket connects at /api/ws/{user_type}/{token} where user_type can be manager, contractor, tenant, or admin. The frontend shows a 'Live' indicator when connected. Initial connections may fail with code 1006 but the reconnection logic handles this correctly. All notification features including priority indicators (critical=red, important=amber, info=green) are working.
